task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Role-Based Access Control** (Level 8)

Build an API with role-based access. Users have roles (admin/editor/viewer). POST /api/articles creates articles (editors and admins only). DELETE /api/articles/:id deletes (admins only). GET /api/articles lists all (anyone with auth). Viewers can read but not create or delete.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required
  role, required

create a
final_source:
build for javascript backend

database is local memory

owner is 'nobody@example.com'
allow signup and login

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required
  role, required

create an Articles table:
  title, required
  body
  author_id

when user calls POST /api/register sending user_data:
  validate user_data:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required
    role is text, required
  saved_user = save user_data to Users
  send back saved_user with status 201

when user calls POST /api/login sending credentials:
  found_user = look up User with credentials's email
  if found_user is not empty:
    if found_user's password is credentials's password:
      token = 'jwt_' + found_user's id
      send back { token is token } with status 200
    else:
      send back { error is 'Invalid credentials' } with status 401
  else:
    send back { error is 'User not found' } with status 401

when user calls GET /api/articles:
  requires login
  articles = get all Articles
  send back articles with status 200

when user calls POST /api/articles sending article:
  requires login
  guard (caller's role is 'editor' or caller's role is 'admin') or 'Forbidden: Only editors and admins can create articles'
  saved_article = save article to Articles
  send back saved_article with status 201

when user calls DELETE /api/articles/:id:
  requires login
  guard caller's role is 'admin' or 'Forbidden: Only admins can delete articles'
  delete Article with this id
  send back { message is 'Article deleted' } with status 204
